pattern: The best value of an h-by-w block is the larger of selling it whole or splitting it once by every possible horizontal or vertical cut, which a bottom-up grid DP fills in.
hints: Define best(h, w) as the most money obtainable from an h-by-w piece; a listed price gives a lower bound for that exact shape.
Any cutting strategy starts with one straight cut, so best(h, w) is the max over all first horizontal and vertical splits of the two resulting subpieces' values.
Fill a (m+1) x (n+1) table by increasing size: best(h, w) = max(direct price, max over i of best(i,w)+best(h-i,w), max over j of best(h,j)+best(h,w-j)).

edge_inputs.description: only the full board is priced
tall strip with no full-board price

stress.description: small board with random distinct shape prices
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    m = rng.randrange(1, max(2, min(size, 30)) + 1)
    n = rng.randrange(1, max(2, min(size, 30)) + 1)
    shapes = []
    seen = set()
    cnt = rng.randrange(1, m * n + 1)
    while len(shapes) < cnt:
        h = rng.randrange(1, m + 1)
        w = rng.randrange(1, n + 1)
        if (h, w) in seen:
            continue
        seen.add((h, w))
        shapes.append([h, w, rng.randrange(1, 1000001)])
    return [m, n, shapes]
stress.note: kept small because the DP and oracle are O(m*n*(m+n))
